free_text: Parameters of the subthreshold fission structure in
240Pu
Oak Ridge electron linear accelerator
Electrons on tantalum target.
There were 3 samples:
- Pu-240 sample: 12 g of S and 10.23 g of PuO2 which
had 98.47% enrichment in Pu-240;
- Pu-239 sample: Metal alloy, mixture of 98.47% Pu
and 1.07% Al.
- U-238 sample (99.99% U-238) was in metallic form and
used for background subtraction.
Two NE-213 liquid scintillators (10 cm in
diameter by 5 cm thick) mounted on 12.7-cm-diam
photomultipliers (58 AVP) were used as fission neutron
detectors.
20-m flight path was used for the
measurement. Data were taken on samples of 240Pu,
239Pu, and 238U. The efficiency for detecting a fission
event by counting fission neutrons was about 5.0% per
detector. Pulse-shape discrimination (PSD) of the
type developed by Forte was used on the signals from
each detector. The neutron TOF spectrum contained a
gamma-ray background caused by gamma rays that were
mistagged as neutrons in the PSD circuit. This
background was removed from the data by subtracting
from the neutron TOF spectrum a fraction K of the
corresponding gamma-ray TOF spectrum. K was
determined from the ratios of resonance areas in the
gamma-ray TOF spectrum to the corresponding areas in
the neutron TOF spectrum for 20 capture resonances in
238U (10 to 500 eV) and for 6 assumed capture-only
resonances in 240Pu (40 to 140 eV). To normalize
detector efficiency authors used partial half-life
for 240Pu(SF) of 1.4xE11 yrs.
Data corrected for system dead-time, detector
efficiency, flux monitor efficiency. Small correction
for multiple scattering and self-shielding effects
was not made.
converted to reaction formalism
converted to new date formats, lower case.

Approved by author 1976/3/15
Table I, p. 1853, Phys.Rev.C,12,1850,1975
In fitting these data authors used
for the resolution function a convolution of a
Gaussian and an exponential with e-folding widths
(eV) of 1.23E (keV) and 1.11E (keV), respectively.
These parameters were determined by fitting the
shapes of small fission width resonances with this
functional form.
Fission widths determined using reduced neutron widths
given by Kolar and Bockhoff, J.Nuc.En.,22, 299 (1968).
Data errors given are statistical errors on
fission widths plus errors of assumed neutron widths.
Class I resonance parameters.
Fig.2, page 1854; Fig. 1, page 1852 is not
suitable for digitizing.
Neutron spectrum shape monitored by 10B parallel plate
ionization chamber. 240Pu data normalized to 239Pu
fission cross section over the interval from 600 to
1000 eV from ENDF/B-IV.
Class II states.
